steps.title: Find the sentence subject
steps.content: The subject is "Several advantages." The long phrase between em dashes only explains those advantages, so the sentence still needs a main verb after the interruption.

steps.title: Use a finite verb
steps.content: Choice B and choice D are participle phrases, not main verbs. Choice C is a prepositional phrase, so it also cannot complete the clause. The remaining choice supplies the finite verb phrase needed for the complete sentence: "Several advantages have positioned acoustic waves as a promising alternative." The answer is A.
